_backup_note: Snapshot of SyncView Calendar — Reorder (OXd0sUoSJYMspGTF) taken 2026-06-12 during Phase 1 prep of the Sheets→Supabase realtime migration (CALENDAR_REALTIME_MIGRATION.md), before any workflow edits. CAUTION — DRAFT AND ACTIVE VERSIONS DIVERGE on this workflow: the nodes below are the DRAFT (versionId 188e6149-e0ee-4c48-9308-513dc2b88240), a batched values:batchUpdate rewrite that was saved but NEVER PUBLISHED. The LIVE published version (activeVersionId 8a591214-b324-4b3e-acef-d888b0343d79) is still the ORIGINAL per-row googleSheets update loop, preserved verbatim in calendar-reorder.2026-06-10.pre-batch.json in this folder. /webhook/calendar-reorder therefore still executes the per-row loop in production. Any edit-and-publish in Phase 1 will ALSO flip live behavior to this batched draft — decide intentionally. WRITES to Calendar_<slug> (order_index + updated_at); on the Phase 1 dual-write list. No inline secrets (draft uses the googleSheetsOAuth2Api credential; active version uses the googleSheets node credential). n8n cloud's own workflow version history is the authoritative rollback path.
name: SyncView Calendar — Reorder

parameters.jsCode:
const b = $('Build BatchUpdate').first().json;
return [{ json: { ok: true, updated: b.updated, skipped: b.skipped } }];

parameters.content:
## SyncView Calendar — Reorder (batched)

POST /webhook/calendar-reorder — body `{ client, items: [{id, order_index}, …] }`.

Rewrites all order_index (+ updated_at) cells in ONE values:batchUpdate call (3 fixed Google API calls total: header row → id column → batchUpdate) instead of one Sheets update per row. A 20-card drag went from ~20 sequential API calls to 3.

- Unknown ids are skipped and reported in `skipped` — same phantom-row guard as before (no append, ever).
- Missing order_index column → error → FE rolls the drag back.
- Response `{ok, updated, skipped}` is sent AFTER the write completes, so the FE serialized-reorder funnel stays race-free.

Pre-change snapshot: repo n8n-backups/calendar-reorder.2026-06-10.pre-batch.json
